fields.output_contract: Writes chatgpt_staging/in/<request_id>/payload.zip.b64 and chatgpt_staging/in/<request_id>/payload_staging_report.json. Validates ZIP contains apply_manifest.json, rejects ERROR TRUNCATED marker, decodes the written base64 back to a temp ZIP, verifies SHA256 round-trip equality, and reports workflow_dispatch payload path.
fields.input_contract: Requires a local ZIP payload containing apply_manifest.json at archive root. Resolves DCOIR_REPO_ROOT from Machine/System environment unless -RepoRoot is supplied. Optional RequestId must contain only letters, numbers, dot, underscore, or dash. Does not commit or push; operator reviews and commits the generated files.
fields.notes: Updated 2026-05-05: apply-in core skill parity runbook now includes batch-size rule and drift-stop rule. Use operator_tools/github_desktop_lane/docs/CHATGPT_APPLY_IN_CORE_SKILL_PARITY_SYNC.md. Recommended remaining seven-skill sync: 4-skill payload then 3-skill payload; if four-skill batch fails, retry 2+2. One-skill payload only for natural final remainder or explicit operator-approved test/retry.
fields.trigger_conditions: Use when a repo update should be applied through the chatgpt-apply-in GitHub Actions lane; when a payload.zip.b64 file is needed under chatgpt_staging/in/<request_id>/; when a prior apply-in run failed with base64 decode errors, invalid input, or payload truncation; when avoiding long inline connector writes for base64 payloads.
fields.safety_preconditions: Connector staging is allowed when explicitly authorized and validated. For core skill source parity sync payloads: maximum 4 skills per apply-in payload; minimum 2 skills per payload when more than one skill remains; do not fall below 2 skills unless only 1 skill remains naturally after successful prior batches or operator explicitly approves a one-skill test/retry. If drift, repeated rediscovery, lane confusion, or off-track troubleshooting appears, stop and ask for operator help instead of improvising.

fields.output_contract: Produces payload.zip, payload.zip.b64, and payload_report.json. Report includes staging path, hashes, manifest, zip validation, base64 round-trip validation, parts_mode false, and forbidden shape list.
fields.input_contract: Inputs: repo root, request id, allowed root(s), and repo-relative include paths. Output must be one ZIP with apply_manifest.json and files/ at archive root, plus one base64 file named payload.zip.b64. Does not support chunks or parts.
fields.notes: Created after recurring DCOIR apply-in drift. Treat as the discoverable tool row that prevents future sessions from inventing parts/chunk staging. GitHub source: tools/chatgpt_apply_in/build_payload_zip_b64.py. Staging contract: chatgpt_staging/in/README.md.
fields.trigger_conditions: Use whenever a DCOIR task mentions chatgpt-apply-in, chatgpt-in, apply-in workflow, payload.zip.b64, chatgpt_staging/in, skill parity source sync, or GitHub connector staging of an apply-in bundle. This is the preferred builder before staging payload.zip.b64.
fields.safety_preconditions: Do not use payload.zip.b64.parts, part files, chunk_manifest.json, chunk markers, wrapper-root-only zips, unsafe request ids, or payloads with truncation markers. If connector limits are hit, reduce batch size and rebuild a smaller single payload.zip.b64.
fields.tool_name: DCOIR ChatGPT Apply-In Single Payload Builder

fields.output_contract: Standardizes single-file log output with tool name, tool version, logging module version, current directory, log path, phases, terminal-relevant status, resolved paths, JSON-safe objects, error type, stack trace, and next action. Used first by New-DcoirApplyInPayload.ps1.
fields.input_contract: Requires DCOIR_DOWNLOADS_DIR when available; falls back to user Downloads/current directory if needed. Tools importing Dcoir.Logging should call Initialize-DcoirToolLog at startup, use Set-DcoirLogPhase and Write-DcoirLogLine/Write-DcoirLogObject during execution, and use Write-DcoirCaughtError in catch blocks. Return log_path in JSON/output on success and failure.
fields.notes: Logging standard is now CAP verified and discoverable through GitHub docs/policy and Operator Tools Registry. Dcoir.Logging is the module-level logging standard for operator tools. New-DcoirApplyInPayload.ps1 imports Dcoir.Logging and returns log_path. Use this row as the live Airtable discovery record for logging default-enforcement.
fields.trigger_conditions: Use for every DCOIR operator-run PowerShell tool or generated codeblock that performs local execution, repo update staging, diagnostics, validation, capture, export, packaging, or workflow orchestration. Future sessions should treat this as the default logging contract: produce one uploadable log/diagnostic file unless explicitly exempted.
fields.safety_preconditions: Do not rely on screenshots for operator-run diagnostics. Do not create new ad hoc logging helpers when Dcoir.Logging can be imported. Exceptions must be explicit and documented. Logs must not print secret environment values; log presence/source and safe paths only.

fields.output_contract: Creates a ChatGPT-friendly ZIP containing command context, run summary, export manifest, metadata coverage, schema files, table schema files, record files when records are enabled, run log, terminal transcript, and ZIP/captured-files manifests. Full-record validation on 2026-05-03 exported 24 table schemas, 24 record files, and 1132 records with RedactLikelySecrets enabled.
fields.input_contract: Requires Machine/System environment variables DCOIR_REPO_ROOT, DCOIR_DOWNLOADS_DIR, DCOIR_AIRTABLE_TOKEN, and DCOIR_AIRTABLE_BASE_ID. Supports ExportMode Auto/SchemaOnly/BoundedRecords/FullRecords, FullRecordDump, SkipRecords, MaxRecordsPerTable, MetadataScope, ProbeUnsupportedMetadata, TableList, RedactLikelySecrets, and NoZip. Use Local Configuration Registry canonical names only; do not invent alternate Airtable token names. DCOIR Airtable record contents are governed as scrubbed operational state, so default DCOIR full exports should not pass RedactLikelySecrets unless the operator explicitly asks for a redacted sample.
fields.notes: Active Airtable database health export tool. Operator policy correction on 2026-05-03: DCOIR Airtable records are scrubbed upstream, so default full DCOIR export is unredacted unless the operator explicitly requests a redacted sample. Keep token/base environment values non-disclosed. GitHub README and tool_catalog source readback already reflect optional RedactLikelySecrets and unredacted full-export guidance.
fields.trigger_conditions: Use when a DCOIR session needs an Airtable schema, metadata, bounded record sample, or full-record snapshot for database health, queue/plan state, registry, helper-memory, validation, retention, cleanup, or cross-session handoff analysis. Future sessions should find this row via Operator Tools Registry search terms: Airtable, database health, full records, schema, metadata, Local Configuration Registry, Work Items, Plans, Validation Evidence.
fields.safety_preconditions: Read-only Airtable API use only. Do not print or export environment token values. DCOIR Airtable record fields are treated as already scrubbed operational data by operator policy; avoid unnecessary record-field redaction because it hides useful workflow values. Cleanup/deletion recommendations must remain governed through Delete Queue and dependency order. Unsupported metadata surfaces such as automations/extensions/interfaces must be reported as unsupported unless future API scope is added.
fields.version_or_hash: 2026-05-03.5 validated full export; commits 1918ff57fc3ecc0da36c34da17d30f2cb0c28382, 0ba54d64ea3d72c7c71491bdb7cae521b86c7c1c, bb649333b8d77501dfc88057e904ceb5ce0dde11, bc15b48ad20c40e9bcc0035abeab9fe1d3633095

fields.launcher_command: powershell.exe -NoProfile -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -Command "& { $repo=[Environment]::GetEnvironmentVariable('DCOIR_REPO_ROOT','Machine'); $out=[Environment]::GetEnvironmentVariable('DCOIR_DOWNLOADS_DIR','Machine'); if ([string]::IsNullOrWhiteSpace($repo)) { throw 'DCOIR_REPO_ROOT is not set as a Windows System variable.' }; if ([string]::IsNullOrWhiteSpace($out)) { throw 'DCOIR_DOWNLOADS_DIR is not set as a Windows System variable.' }; & (Join-Path $repo 'operator_tools\github_desktop_lane\scripts\Invoke-DcoirRepoPatchApply.ps1') -ManifestJson (Join-Path $out 'dcoir_apply_manifest.json') -PayloadRoot (Join-Path $out 'dcoir_payload') }"
fields.output_contract: Writes dcoir_repo_patch_apply_*.log.txt and dcoir_repo_patch_apply_*.result.json. Result JSON includes copied/deleted arrays, warnings, status, and what_if_only.
fields.input_contract: Requires ManifestJson and PayloadRoot. Manifest must define allowed_target_roots and copy_map or delete_paths. Supports WhatIfOnly, SkipFetch, AllowDirtyTree, expected_branch, pre/post hash checks, and payload_root_policy.
fields.notes: Registry refreshed after validated module split. Backing modules: Dcoir.Git and Dcoir.RepoPatch. Validation evidence: WhatIf pass recq5syy0NiN1khUd, warning-clean pass recJMd8zAJQl4XlWN, real fixture apply pass recNLY6pkglPqAG6z.
fields.trigger_conditions: Use when ChatGPT provides a payload folder or extracted ZIP plus an explicit repo-relative apply manifest; repeated repair/apply bundles; wrapper-root/numeric-root cleanup; explicit copy-map and delete-path application; safe local GitHub Desktop review before commit.
fields.safety_preconditions: Uses Dcoir.Git and Dcoir.RepoPatch. Validates branch, ahead/behind, dirty tree, allowed roots, path safety, hashes, postchecks, and final git status. Use WhatIfOnly before real apply unless intentionally applying reviewed payload.

fields.launcher_command: Double-click Run_DCOIR_WorkItemsSchemaCleanup_DryRun.cmd first. Then run ApplyOptions, ApplySafe, Verify, and GenerateOptionDeleteScript as needed.
fields.output_contract: Writes timestamped logs and JSON/Markdown reports to DCOIR_DOWNLOADS_DIR or Downloads. Safe modes normalize values and prefix retired fields. Dangerous modes require explicit confirmation words.
fields.input_contract: Requires local environment variable DCOIR_AIRTABLE_TOKEN or AIRTABLE_TOKEN. Optional: DCOIR_AIRTABLE_BASE_ID, DCOIR_AIRTABLE_WORK_ITEMS_TABLE_ID, DCOIR_DOWNLOADS_DIR. Run dry-run first before apply modes.
fields.notes: Work Items cleanup tool v7 prepared after final cleanup verification. Current Work Items cleanup no longer expects retired DELETE-prefixed fields. README, Python constants, generic design note, and example config now reflect that retired fields and Queue Control active_work_items link were removed. Future generic cleanup should use config files rather than table-specific environment variables.
fields.trigger_conditions: Use when simplifying the Airtable Work Items table, normalizing Status/Area/Work Type values, prefixing retired fields with DELETE -, generating option-delete scripts, or testing API deletion capability.
fields.safety_preconditions: Do not run apply-safe until dry-run is reviewed. Do not run dangerous delete attempts unless the operator explicitly approves. Field/option deletion may not be supported by Airtable Web API; use generated Scripting Extension fallback where needed.
fields.tool_name: DCOIR Work Items Schema Cleanup Tool

fields.launcher_command: powershell.exe -NoProfile -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -Command "& { $repo=[Environment]::GetEnvironmentVariable('DCOIR_REPO_ROOT','Machine'); if ([string]::IsNullOrWhiteSpace($repo)) { throw 'DCOIR_REPO_ROOT is not set as a Windows System variable.' }; & (Join-Path $repo 'operator_tools\github_desktop_lane\scripts\Invoke-DcoirSafePrePullApply.ps1') }"
fields.output_contract: Writes dcoir_safe_prepull_apply_*.txt. Captures before/after status, stash behavior, fetch, fast-forward pull, reapply result, and stash list.
fields.input_contract: Requires DCOIR_REPO_ROOT and DCOIR_DOWNLOADS_DIR Machine/System environment variables. Runs locally before pulling when local work may exist.
fields.notes: Registry refreshed after validated module split. Backing module: Dcoir.Git. Validation evidence: VE-20260501-DCOIR-SAFE-PREPULL-SMOKE-PASS / recMvHl1lxNvVOFpu.
fields.trigger_conditions: Use when the repo is behind origin/main and local edits block a fast-forward pull, especially before committing a GitHub Desktop bundle.
fields.safety_preconditions: Uses Dcoir.Git. Stashes current work, fast-forward pulls, and reapplies only a newly-created stash. Preserves existing older stashes and does not use git stash pop.

fields.output_contract: Writes timestamped log and JSON report to DCOIR_DOWNLOADS_DIR. Expected marker: DCOIR_CLOSE_GITHUB_ISSUES_DONE.
fields.input_contract: Requires DCOIR_GITHUB_FG_TOKEN or DCOIR_GITHUB_CL_TOKEN local environment variable. Defaults to dry-run. Apply requires explicit -Apply and -ConfirmCloseAll. Optional Owner, Repo, TokenEnvName, StateReason, CloseComment, and MaxIssues parameters.
fields.notes: Created during OpenAI API/env setup branch after operator provided GitHub token environment variable names. Tool is intentionally dry-run-first and guarded for bulk issue closure.
fields.trigger_conditions: Use when the operator explicitly asks to close all open GitHub issues in the dcoir-collector repository or needs a safe dry-run/apply issue close tool using local GitHub token environment variables.
fields.safety_preconditions: Destructive issue-state change. Always run dry-run first and review output before apply. Excludes pull requests. Never prints token values. Stop if issue count or repo target is unexpected.

fields.launcher_command: powershell.exe -NoProfile -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -Command "& { $repo=[Environment]::GetEnvironmentVariable('DCOIR_REPO_ROOT','Machine'); if ([string]::IsNullOrWhiteSpace($repo)) { throw 'DCOIR_REPO_ROOT is not set as a Windows System variable.' }; & (Join-Path $repo 'operator_tools\github_desktop_lane\scripts\New-DcoirTextOnlyRepoSnapshot.ps1') }"
fields.output_contract: Writes dcoir_text_only_repo_snapshot_*.zip, *.log.txt, and *.manifest.json. Captures included/skipped files and reasons.
fields.input_contract: Requires DCOIR_REPO_ROOT and DCOIR_DOWNLOADS_DIR. Optional SnapshotName, MaxFileBytes, exclude lists, IncludeDotDirectories, and NoGitCleanCheck.
fields.notes: Registry refreshed after validated module split. Backing modules: Dcoir.Git and Dcoir.Snapshot. Validation evidence: VE-20260501-DCOIR-SNAPSHOT-TEXTONLY-SMOKE-PASS / reccoGUzBAqrKk6CG.
fields.trigger_conditions: Use when ChatGPT needs a full local dcoir-collector repo snapshot for text/reference scanning while excluding binaries, generated dependency folders, and oversized files.
fields.safety_preconditions: Read-only repo text snapshot. Uses Dcoir.Git and Dcoir.Snapshot. Excludes binaries, generated/dependency folders, and oversized files; binary-sniffs remaining files.

fields.launcher_command: powershell.exe -NoProfile -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -Command "& { $repo=[Environment]::GetEnvironmentVariable('DCOIR_REPO_ROOT','Machine'); if ([string]::IsNullOrWhiteSpace($repo)) { throw 'DCOIR_REPO_ROOT is not set as a Machine/System environment variable.' }; & (Join-Path $repo 'operator_tools\github_desktop_lane\scripts\Invoke-DcoirActionsWorkflowOrchestrator.ps1') }"
fields.output_contract: Writes orchestrator logs, manifests, run summaries, job/log capture, optional downloaded artifacts, optional ChatGPT-friendly ZIP, orchestrator_summary.json, and orchestrator_summary.md depending on manifest settings.
fields.input_contract: Requires DCOIR_REPO_ROOT, DCOIR_DOWNLOADS_DIR, GitHub CLI availability/auth, and a reviewed JSON manifest. Supports watch/capture/dispatch modes, guarded live dispatch, max_dispatch_count, max_parallel, fail_fast, artifact download, ChatGPT-friendly ZIP packaging, cleanup_output_folder_after_zip, and capture controls.
fields.notes: Registry refreshed after validated orchestrator and module split. Backing modules: Dcoir.Actions, Dcoir.GitHub, Dcoir.Packaging, Dcoir.Common, and compatibility facade DcoirActionsOrchestrator. Promotion evidence includes solo, ladder, parallel, and FullRegression gates under WI-20260430-REVIEW-ACTIONS-POWERSHELL-TOOLS. Preferred Actions lane remains promoted.
fields.trigger_conditions: Use when the operator needs to watch existing GitHub Actions workflow runs, capture known run IDs, or dispatch and monitor a manifest-defined batch of 1..N workflow runs with different inputs/settings. Trigger on workflow run monitoring, GitHub Actions evidence capture, live validation runs, dispatch workflow, watch workflow, capture run logs, run multiple validation suites, or create a ChatGPT-friendly Actions evidence ZIP.
fields.safety_preconditions: Preferred Actions operator lane. Orchestrator/modules own dispatch, monitor, fail-fast gates, parallel workflow execution, capture, artifact download, ChatGPT-friendly ZIP packaging, cleanup, summaries, and exit codes. Harnesses may only create JSON config and execute this entrypoint.

fields.output_contract: Produces Actions orchestrator smoke outputs such as dcoir_actions_workflow_orchestrator_sample.chatgpt.zip, watch sample ZIPs, live dispatch test ZIPs/logs/manifests depending on mode.
fields.input_contract: Harness-only wrapper. Creates guarded smoke manifests for dry-run/watch/live validation and executes Invoke-DcoirActionsWorkflowOrchestrator.ps1. Requires DCOIR_REPO_ROOT, DCOIR_DOWNLOADS_DIR, GitHub CLI auth, and reviewed manifest settings for live dispatch.
fields.notes: Registry refreshed after validated orchestrator and module split. Actions orchestrator lane promoted as preferred. Harness/wrapper limit preference recorded as PREF-HARNESS-WRAPPER-LIMITS-MODULE-FIRST / recVmb1FODvwVxQMu.
fields.trigger_conditions: Use when the operator wants a set-and-forget validation launcher for the standard Actions orchestrator smoke path: dry-run validation, watch validation, and one guarded live dispatch. Trigger on wrapper, smoke runner, one command Actions validation, set-and-forget workflow test, or avoiding long PowerShell paste blocks.
fields.safety_preconditions: Harness must remain thin. It may create JSON config and invoke the orchestrator only. Durable dispatch/monitor/capture/cleanup/packaging behavior belongs to the orchestrator/modules.

fields.launcher_command: powershell.exe -NoProfile -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -Command "& { $repo=[Environment]::GetEnvironmentVariable('DCOIR_REPO_ROOT','Machine'); $out=[Environment]::GetEnvironmentVariable('DCOIR_DOWNLOADS_DIR','Machine'); if ([string]::IsNullOrWhiteSpace($repo)) { throw 'DCOIR_REPO_ROOT is not set as a Windows System variable.' }; if ([string]::IsNullOrWhiteSpace($out)) { throw 'DCOIR_DOWNLOADS_DIR is not set as a Windows System variable.' }; & (Join-Path $repo 'operator_tools\github_desktop_lane\scripts\New-DcoirChatGPTFriendlyZip.ps1') -SourceFolder (Join-Path $out 'some_diagnostic_folder') -OutputZip (Join-Path $out 'some_diagnostic_folder.chatgpt.zip') -NormalizeTextEncoding }"
fields.output_contract: Writes rootless ChatGPT-friendly ZIP with diagnostic_index.md, captured_files.json, and zip_manifest.json prioritized for fast triage.
fields.input_contract: Requires DCOIR_REPO_ROOT and DCOIR_DOWNLOADS_DIR. Takes SourceFolder and OutputZip; optional NormalizeTextEncoding and IndexTitle.
fields.notes: Registry refreshed after validated module split. Duplicate/related ZIP helper registry row retained pending future registry dedupe through Delete Queue if desired.
fields.trigger_conditions: Use when a DCOIR diagnostic, snapshot, evidence tool, or ad hoc/on-the-fly copy-paste terminal script needs to create an upload ZIP that is easier for ChatGPT to unzip, inspect, and parse. Trigger on upload-friendly ZIP, chatgpt-friendly ZIP, diagnostics ZIP, captured_files.json, zip_manifest.json, UTF-8 normalization, hidden/system metadata cleanup, wrapper-root cleanup, repeated terminal-log upload friction, or any script that produces files the operator will upload back into ChatGPT.
fields.safety_preconditions: Packaging helper only. Avoids wrapper-root junk, skips hidden/system metadata, writes forward-slash archive paths, and can normalize staged text copies without modifying originals.

fields.notes: Active ChatGPT exec lane. Validated direct exec and apply-in-to-exec handoff. Exact learned sequence: create apply-in payload that writes one exec request JSON, verify chatgpt-apply-in report, verify exec request readback, then if no exec report appears because apply-in used [skip ci], use GitHub connector update_file on the already-created exec request JSON with a metadata-only restaged_at_utc update and non-[skip ci] commit message. Preserve the approved command and approved_command_preview exactly. Validated example: apply-20260503-exec-hashfix-validation-001 -> update_file on chatgpt_staging/exec_requests/exec-20260503-airtable-schema-hashfix-002.json -> restage commit 5fdfe97b6f4296de2048a6330a9f8fb76d2b4a5e -> exec run 25287957928. Hash-fallback validation passed: output_zip was non-null.

fields.output_contract: Produces dcoir_actions_ladder-*.chatgpt.zip and dcoir_actions_mode_ladder_result.json, plus orchestrator logs/manifests for each gate.
fields.input_contract: Harness-only fail-fast same-workflow mode/suite ladder wrapper. Creates sequential ladder manifest and executes Invoke-DcoirActionsWorkflowOrchestrator.ps1. Requires reviewed workflow/mode list and GitHub CLI auth for live dispatch.
fields.notes: Registry refreshed after validated orchestrator and module split. Used for fail-fast mode ladder gates. Harness/wrapper limit preference recorded as PREF-HARNESS-WRAPPER-LIMITS-MODULE-FIRST / recVmb1FODvwVxQMu.
fields.trigger_conditions: Use for sequential same-workflow mode or suite validation where lower-intensity gates must pass before heavier modes run. Trigger on fail-fast ladder, low-to-high suite ladder, manual-full-validation modes, or FullRegression-last validation.
fields.safety_preconditions: Harness must remain thin. It may create JSON config and execute the orchestrator only. Sequential fail-fast behavior is implemented by orchestrator/modules, not ad hoc wrapper workarounds.

fields.launcher_command: powershell.exe -NoProfile -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -Command "& { $repo=[Environment]::GetEnvironmentVariable('DCOIR_REPO_ROOT','Machine'); if ([string]::IsNullOrWhiteSpace($repo)) { throw 'DCOIR_REPO_ROOT is not set as a Windows System variable.' }; & (Join-Path $repo 'operator_tools\github_desktop_lane\scripts\New-DcoirTargetedSnapshot.ps1') -ManifestJson (Join-Path $repo 'operator_tools\github_desktop_lane\manifests\docs_impl_snapshot.sample.json') }"
fields.output_contract: Writes targeted snapshot ZIP and log in DCOIR_DOWNLOADS_DIR. Copies only requested repo-relative paths and fails on missing or unsafe paths.
fields.input_contract: Requires a JSON manifest with name and repo-relative paths. Requires DCOIR_REPO_ROOT and DCOIR_DOWNLOADS_DIR. Performs branch, clean-tree, fetch, and fast-forward checks before copying.
fields.notes: Registry refreshed after validated module split. Backing modules: Dcoir.Git and Dcoir.Snapshot. Validation evidence: VE-20260501-DCOIR-SNAPSHOT-TARGETED-SMOKE-PASS / recj7JZy2a5Cq8Tqv.
fields.trigger_conditions: Use when the assistant needs a targeted ZIP snapshot of selected repo paths instead of a whole repo snapshot.
fields.safety_preconditions: Read-only snapshot except output folder writes. Uses Dcoir.Git for freshness checks and Dcoir.Snapshot for path safety/copying. Missing requested paths are hard failures.

fields.launcher_command: powershell.exe -NoProfile -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -Command "& { $repo=[Environment]::GetEnvironmentVariable('DCOIR_REPO_ROOT','Machine'); if ([string]::IsNullOrWhiteSpace($repo)) { throw 'DCOIR_REPO_ROOT is not set as a Windows System variable.' }; & (Join-Path $repo 'operator_tools\github_desktop_lane\scripts\Get-DcoirGitConflictDiagnostic.ps1') }"
fields.output_contract: Writes dcoir_git_conflict_diagnostic_*.txt in DCOIR_DOWNLOADS_DIR. Captures branch, HEAD, remotes, fetch, status, ahead/behind, unmerged files, diff summaries, stash list, merge/rebase/cherry-pick state, and recent graph.
fields.input_contract: Requires DCOIR_REPO_ROOT and DCOIR_DOWNLOADS_DIR Machine/System environment variables. Runs locally in Windows PowerShell 5.1 or PowerShell 7. No manifest required.
fields.notes: Registry refreshed after validated module split. Backing module: Dcoir.Git. Validation evidence: VE-20260501-DCOIR-GIT-DIAGNOSTIC-SMOKE-PASS / recuDTjZAg85Q7e9m.
fields.trigger_conditions: Use when GitHub Desktop shows a weird error, commit/push/pull fails, local repo state is unclear, or the assistant needs a complete git state log before recommending a fix.
fields.safety_preconditions: Read-only diagnostic. Uses Dcoir.Git. Safe for conflict investigation. Does not run destructive git commands.
